98, The handler currently accepts the recording Blob (variable recording) and
later buffers it into memory without a size cap; add an explicit max upload size
check immediately after extracting recording from formData (before any buffering
or recording.arrayBuffer() calls) by introducing a MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES constant and
returning an appropriate error response (e.g., NextResponse.json with a 413/400
status and clear message) if recording.size exceeds the limit; ensure the check
is placed right after the recording/roomId validation block that currently
returns for missing/empty blobs so oversized uploads are rejected early and do
not get fully buffered.

In `@packages/core/src/coherence/server/signal-orchestrator.ts` around lines 28 -
60, The constants like WINDOW_MINUTES, MAX_ATTEMPTS, MIN_RELEVANCE, etc. can
become NaN if env vars are invalid; add a small helper (e.g., parseEnvNumber or
parseFiniteNumber) that reads the string, coerces to Number, checks
Number.isFinite(value) (and optionally Math.floor for integer-only values), and
returns a safe default when invalid, then replace direct Number(...) uses for
HYPHA_SIGNAL_ORCHESTRATOR_* with this helper so all guardrail constants always
have finite fallback values.
body_head:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
**Harden numeric env parsing before using these guardrails.**
An invalid `HYPHA_SIGNAL_ORCHESTRATOR_*` value turns these constants into `NaN`. That breaks scheduling (`dueAt` becomes `Invalid Date`) and makes the threshold checks silently misbehave. Please coerce each env var through a helper that falls back when the parsed value is not finite, instead of using `Number(...)` directly.
<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

title: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_
body_head:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_
**Make pending-job dedupe atomic.**
This `findFirst` → `update/insert` sequence is racy. Two concurrent events for the same space can both miss `pending`, both insert, and then the orchestrator evaluates the same space twice. This needs a DB-backed guarantee, e.g. a partial unique index for pending rows plus an upsert/transactional retry path.

In `@packages/core/src/governance/server/call-artifacts.ts` around lines 74 - 108,
The mediaUri from input.recording is being persisted verbatim (used in the
insert into spaceCallRecordings); validate and normalize it before the DB write:
parse input.recording.mediaUri and allow only trusted schemes/origins (e.g. mxc:
scheme or HTTPS host allowlist), reject or return an error for anything else, or
instead store only an opaque storageKey and resolve the actual fetch URL
server-side when serving recordings; implement this check immediately prior to
the insert/onConflict block that writes to spaceCallRecordings (use
input.recording.mediaUri, input.recording.storageKey, and callSessionId to guide
handling).
body_head:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_
**Validate `recording.mediaUri` against trusted schemes/origins before storing it.**
This field is persisted verbatim, and the new org-memory fetch path later performs a server-side GET against it for call recordings. That turns arbitrary `mediaUri` values into an SSRF primitive. Please restrict recordings to trusted storage locations only (for example `mxc://` or an allowlisted HTTPS origin), or store an opaque storage key and resolve the fetch URL server-side.
<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

In `@packages/core/src/matrix/client/hooks/call-recording.ts` around lines 43 -
58, The current recognition.onresult handler iterates from 0 over the cumulative
results and re-adds previously finalized transcripts into chunks; change the
loop to start at event.resultIndex (use the incoming event.resultIndex value)
and iterate only over new entries in the results/list so only newly delivered,
final results are pushed into the chunks array; update references in the handler
where it reads e.results/list and result.isFinal to use the slice starting at
event.resultIndex to avoid duplicate transcripts.
body_head:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>
🌐 Web query:
`In the Web Speech API, does SpeechRecognitionEvent.results contain the cumulative result list on each onresult callback, and should SpeechRecognitionEvent.resultIndex be used to process only new results and avoid duplicate final transcripts?`
💡 Result:
Yes, the SpeechRecognitionEvent.results property contains the cumulative list of all speech recognition results for the current session [1][2]. This list includes all final results returned so far, followed by the current best hypothesis for any interim results [1]. Because the results property is cumulative, you should use the SpeechRecognitionEvent.resultIndex property to efficiently process only the new or changed results [3][4]. The resultIndex property returns the lowest index in the SpeechRecognitionResultList that has actually changed [3][4]. By starting your processing loop from this index, you can avoid re-processing previous results and prevent the duplication of final transcripts [4]. When handling these events, keep in mind that interim results may be updated or removed as the recognition service refines its hypotheses [1]. Final results, however, are immutable once they are marked as final [1].
Citations:

```diff
-      const activeRoomId = roomId?.trim();
+      const activeRoomId =
